editableNewDialogue.text: i’m making a little trivia night—give me three quick “common misconception” questions with answers

1) Q: Which is closer to the Earth: the Moon or the Sun? A: The Moon.
2) Q: Do goldfish only have a 3-second memory? A: No—studies suggest they can remember things for weeks.
3) Q: Is the Great Wall of China visible from space with the naked eye? A: Generally no; it’s hard to see without aid and depends on conditions.
nice—can you make them a bit harder?

1) Q: What’s the name for the “tongue map” idea that different tongue regions taste only sweet/salty/sour/bitter? A: A misconception; taste receptors for those tastes are distributed across the tongue.
2) Q: What’s wrong with “we only use 10% of our brain”? A: It’s false; brain imaging shows many regions are active over a typical day, even at rest.
3) Q: Does cracking knuckles cause arthritis? A: Evidence doesn’t show a causal link; it may cause temporary swelling or reduced grip strength in some cases.
switch topics—what’s a simple way to explain why the sky is blue?

Sunlight has many colors. Earth’s air scatters shorter blue wavelengths more than longer red wavelengths (Rayleigh scattering), so blue light gets spread across the sky and reaches your eyes from every direction.
give me one random weird fact
In 1958, the US briefly considered detonating nuclear bombs on the Moon (a never-executed proposal sometimes referred to as “Project A119”) to study the explosion and make a visible display.
that low-carb list helps—just keep anything with tonic out, it always throws my carbs off.

Got it—skipping tonic makes sense since it often has added sugar. If you want low-carb mixers, soda water (sparkling water), diet/zero soft drinks, or just water with citrus are usually the easiest swaps. If you tell me what spirit you like (vodka, gin, tequila, etc.), I can suggest a few simple combinations without tonic.
hey quick correction from earlier — i’m not actually looking for low‑carb alcohol options. i asked that for a mate who’s doing keto. i don’t drink.
Got it—so that question wasn’t about your own preferences. Since you don’t drink, I won’t treat that as an interest in low‑carb alcoholic drinks.
yeah, exactly. it wasn’t for me.
